economic_notes: Willow Cree Developments Limited Partnership established for economic development purposes. COVID-19 impacted operations through social distancing, reduced gaming revenues, and band office closure. 38 contaminated sites identified and being assessed.
notes: Two RBC loans totaling $2,783,712 are in default due to termination of professional services firm relationship; contingency claims of $1,275,713 plus potential additional contingency fees from former professional services provider disputing unpaid fees for Specific Claims representation. Emphasis of matter paragraph in auditor report references this dispute. Willow Cree Developments Limited Partnership accounted for by modified equity method (1 GBE). Consolidated entities include Willow Cree Health Services Corporation, CMHC Housing Program, and Beardy's & Okemasis Legacy Trust. ISC COVID-19 funding significantly increased revenue in 2021. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($21,034,242) + FNIHB ($4,146,952) + CMHC ($1,935,433) + Prairie Spirit School Division ($546,010) + Government of Saskatchewan ($146,931) + Other government agencies ($370,198) + Public Health Agency of Canada ($88,514) + Saskatchewan Ministry of Justice ($57,235) + Department of Justice Canada ($39,577) + Correction Services Canada ($215,944) + Treaty Six Education Council ($48,184) + Department of Justice RCMP rent ($30,592) + FSIN ($107,594) = $28,767,406 approximate; OSR includes lease income, rental income, BHP Billiton, investment income, business entity earnings, daycare fees, SITAG, CSC, QBOW CFS, College of Physicians, First Nations Trust, and other revenues.
